title: Quick Friday Fish
excerpt: At our house Fridays were always fish days. I used stockfish (hake) for this dish but you can use any firm white fish. And yes, I used a soup powder. (the food snobs turn up their noses at soup powder). The secret of the dish is not to overcook the fish. …
html:
At our house Fridays were always fish days. I used stockfish (hake) for this dish but you can use any firm white fish. And yes, I used a soup powder. ( the food snobs turn up their noses at soup powder). The secret of the dish is not to overcook the fish.

YOU NEED:

  • 3 fish pieces ( I used frozen fish and let it thaw beforehand)
  • 250 ml cream
  • 1/2 packet white onion soup powder
  • 1 punnet mushrooms
  • fish spice if you have it
  • salt and pepper
  • 250 ml strong cheese
  • 1 small wheel of feta

NOW:

  1. cut the fish into portions and place in your oven dish
  2. sprinkle your salt, pepper and fish spice over
  3. sprinkle the soup powder over
  4. slice the mushrooms and scatter over the fish
  5. pour your tub of cream over
  6. cover the fish with foil and bake in the oven for 15 minutes at 180 degrees Celsius
  7. after 15 minutes take off the foil and sprinkle your cheese over the fish
  8. now bake uncovered for another 15 minutes

Enjoy the dish with chips, vegetables or rice. Open a lovely bottle of cold white wine and ease into the weekend.
